de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.merge.procedures

Class MergeByIfThenElse

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.merge.MergeProcedure
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.merge.procedures.MergeByIfThenElse
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    UnparametricMergeProcedure

    public class MergeByIfThenElse
extends MergeProcedure
implements UnparametricMergeProcedure
    Rule that merges two sequents based on the if-then-else construction: If two locations are assigned different values in the states, the value in the merged state is chosen based on the path condition. This rule retains total precision. The if-then-else distinction is realized by the respective construct for the update / symbolic state of the symbolic execution state. Note: Doing this not with updates, but in the antecedent / path condition can be much more efficient: See MergeIfThenElseAntecedent.
    See Also:
    MergeIfThenElseAntecedent, MergeRule

    • Method Detail

      • instance

        public static MergeByIfThenElse instance()

      • complete

        public boolean complete()
        Description copied from class: MergeProcedure
        Similar to AbstractBuiltInRuleApp.complete(). Method was introduced for predicate abstraction (which is not complete if the abstraction predicates are not set).
        Specified by:
        complete in class MergeProcedure
        Returns:
        true iff the merge procedure is complete (all neede parameters are set).

      • mergeValuesInStates

        public MergeProcedure.ValuesMergeResult mergeValuesInStates(Term v,
                                                            SymbolicExecutionState state1,
                                                            Term valueInState1,
                                                            SymbolicExecutionState state2,
                                                            Term valueInState2,
                                                            Term distinguishingFormula,
                                                            Services services)
        Description copied from class: MergeProcedure
        Merges two values valueInState1 and valueInState2 of corresponding SE states state1 and state2 to a new value of a merge state.
        Specified by:
        mergeValuesInStates in class MergeProcedure
        Parameters:
        v - The variable for which the values should be merged
        state1 - First SE state.
        valueInState1 - Value in state1.
        state2 - Second SE state.
        valueInState2 - Value in state2.
        distinguishingFormula - The user-specified distinguishing formula. May be null (for automatic generation).
        services - The services object.
        Returns:
        The merge result.

      • requiresDistinguishablePathConditions

        public boolean requiresDistinguishablePathConditions()
        Specified by:
        requiresDistinguishablePathConditions in class MergeProcedure
        Returns:
        true iff the merge procedure requires distinguishable path conditions. This is usually the case for procedures working with concrete values of input states, and can be false for abstraction methods.

      • createIfThenElseTerm

        public static Term createIfThenElseTerm(SymbolicExecutionState state1,
                                        SymbolicExecutionState state2,
                                        Term ifTerm,
                                        Term elseTerm,
                                        Term distinguishingFormula,
                                        Services services)
        Creates an if-then-else term for the variable v. If t1 is the right side for v in state1, and t2 is the right side in state1, the resulting term corresponds to \if (c1) \then (t1) \else (t2), where c1 is the path condition of state1. However, the method also tries an optimization: The path condition c2 of state2 could be used if it is shorter than c1. Moreover, equal parts of c1 and c2 could be omitted, since the condition shall only distinguish between the states.
        Parameters:
        state1 - First state to evaluate.
        state2 - Second state to evaluate.
        ifTerm - The term t1 (in the context of state1).
        elseTerm - The term t2 (in the context of state2).
        distinguishingFormula - The user-specified distinguishing formula. May be null (for automatic generation).
        services - The services object.
        Returns:
        An if then else term like \if (c1) \then (t1) \else (t2), where the cI are the path conditions of stateI.

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
